name: United Kingdom (pre-1801 Union Flag)

colors.symbolism: the blue field, from the Scottish saltire of St Andrew
the saltire of St Andrew and the fimbriation of St George's Cross
the Cross of St George of England

description: The original Union Flag: the white saltire of St Andrew on a blue field, overlaid by the red Cross of St George fimbriated white. It lacks the red saltire of St Patrick added in 1801.
history: Adopted on 12 April 1606 by King James VI and I to combine the flags of England and Scotland under the Union of the Crowns, and used as the flag of the Kingdom of Great Britain after the 1707 Acts of Union. It was replaced on 1 January 1801 by the modern Union Jack, which added the Cross of St Patrick on the union with Ireland.

features.symbolism: the original Union Flag superimposing the Cross of St George (England) on the Saltire of St Andrew (Scotland), without the later Cross of St Patrick
